name: Elastic Load Balancing
summary: Production monitoring, observability, and security adoption pack for Application Load Balancers and target groups.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Observability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Track traffic, latency, target health, response codes, TLS posture, and routing health.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Explainability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Use access logs, target health reason codes, and request traces to explain routing failures.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Quality
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Measure availability, error rate, latency SLOs, and backend health.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Ethics and Safety
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Protect entry points with TLS, WAF, security groups, and least-exposed listeners.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Continuous Validation
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Continuously validate listener rules, certificates, target health, and public exposure.

sections.name: Traffic Overview
sections.purpose: Request count, active connections, processed bytes, and traffic trend.

sections.name: Target Health
sections.purpose: Healthy and unhealthy target count, reason codes, and target group distribution.

sections.name: Latency
sections.purpose: Target response time, p95 latency, and slow target identification.

sections.name: HTTP Errors
sections.purpose: ELB 4xx/5xx, target 4xx/5xx, rejected connections, and routing failures.

sections.name: Security Edge
sections.purpose: TLS policy, certificate expiry process, WAF blocks, and public listener exposure.

sections.name: Access Logs
sections.purpose: Request path, client IP, target, status code, and user agent analysis.

log_queries.name: ALB 5xx access log entries
log_queries.intent: Find requests returning target or ELB 5xx.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, elb_status_code, target_status_code, request_url, target_ip, user_agent | filter elb_status_code like /^5/ or target_status_code like /^5/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: ALB slow requests
log_queries.intent: Find requests with high target processing time.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, target_processing_time, request_url, target_ip, elb_status_code, target_status_code | filter target_processing_time > 1 | sort target_processing_time desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: ALB WAF blocked requests
log_queries.intent: Find WAF-blocked requests if WAF logs are centralized.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, action, terminatingRuleId, httpRequest.clientIp, httpRequest.uri | filter action = "BLOCK" | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

alarms.name: ALBTarget5xxHigh
alarms.action: Inspect target group health, backend logs, and recent deployments.

alarms.name: ALBElb5xxHigh
alarms.action: Inspect load balancer configuration, capacity signals, and AWS service health.

alarms.name: ALBTargetLatencyHigh
alarms.action: Inspect slow targets and downstream dependencies.

alarms.name: ALBUnhealthyTargets
alarms.action: Investigate health check path, target port, security groups, and app startup.

security_controls.name: TLS listener policy
security_controls.evidence: HTTPS listeners use approved TLS policies and managed certificates.

security_controls.name: WAF association
security_controls.evidence: Public ALBs are associated with AWS WAF where internet-facing.

security_controls.name: Access logs
security_controls.evidence: ALB access logs are enabled to a controlled S3 bucket with retention.

security_controls.name: Security group path
security_controls.evidence: Only required listener ports are exposed and target access is restricted to the ALB security group.

security_controls.name: CloudTrail auditability
security_controls.evidence: Listener, target group, certificate, and security group changes are visible in CloudTrail.

adoption_plan: Enable ALB access logs for internet-facing load balancers.
Create dashboard sections for traffic, latency, target health, and HTTP errors.
Create alarms for target 5xx, ELB 5xx, latency, and unhealthy targets.
Review TLS policy, certificate ownership, WAF association, and security groups.
Promote the pack as the standard for ALB-backed services.
